items.headline: City launches financial assistance program for water customers
items.summary: San José approved a pilot program to provide financial assistance to water customers, likely aimed at helping residents struggling with water bills. The program addresses affordability concerns as water rates continue to be a burden for many households.

items.headline: 27-unit townhome project with affordable housing proposed
items.summary: The Planning Commission reviewed a residential development featuring 27 three-story townhomes, including 5 units designated as affordable housing. The project represents a mixed-income addition to Cupertino's housing stock.

items.headline: Lawsuit filed against city and SummerHill Homes over development dispute
items.summary: A case (Mark Fantozzi v. City of Cupertino and SummerHill Homes) is in active litigation involving what appears to be a residential development dispute. The city council discussed the case in closed session on May 11.

items.headline: City Council considers 72-unit affordable housing on downtown parking lot
items.summary: The council held a public hearing on a proposed six-story, 100% affordable housing project at 450 Lytton Avenue that would replace a 52-space city-owned surface parking lot with 72 residential units. The project is seeking density bonus concessions under state law to make the development financially feasible.

items.headline: Major electrical infrastructure upgrade permitted for Porter Drive R&D campus
items.summary: Palo Alto has permitted significant electrical infrastructure improvements at 3210 Porter Drive to support new research and development operations. The project includes new transformers, switches, and conduit extending to a substation on Hanover Drive, signaling substantial new R&D tenant activity in the area.

items.headline: New single-family home permitted in San José under SB9 rules
items.summary: A new detached single-family home has been permitted at 326 Battle Dance Drive under California's SB9 streamlined approval process, which allows property owners to split lots and build additional homes with reduced zoning restrictions. This represents San José's continued implementation of state housing legislation designed to increase residential supply.

items.headline: Campbell adopts policy restricting use of city property for federal immigration enforcement
items.summary: The City Council passed a resolution prohibiting city-owned and city-operated property from being used for federal civil immigration enforcement activities. This policy shields city facilities and resources from being made available to federal immigration authorities.

items.headline: City declares hazardous vegetation a public nuisance
items.summary: Los Gatos adopted a resolution formally declaring overgrown brush and hazardous vegetation a public nuisance, enabling the city to order property owners to clear dangerous vegetation. This gives the city enforcement authority to address fire risk and safety concerns on private properties.
